spread_008.text:
and how.

The major advances in civilization are processes that all but wreck the societies in which they occur.

-- A. N. Whitehead, 1933

We find ourselves in a pre-paradigmatic moment in which our technology has outpaced our theories of what to do with it.

-- Benjamin Bratton, 2025

spread_009.text:
Before you opened this book, a model chose the news you read this morning. A model composed three emails you believed were written by colleagues. A model decided which faces you would find attractive, which restaurants you would consider, which words would autocomplete your sentences. A model scored your creditworthiness, flagged your face at the airport, and generated the background music in the cafe where you are perhaps reading this now.

You did not ask for any of this. You did not consent. You were not consulted.

The model, or process, of our time -- computational intelligence -- is reshaping cognition, creativity, and every pattern of epistemic life. Not by announcing itself. Not by arriving. But by having already arrived, everywhere, silently, before the question of whether it should could even be posed.

When humans and cognitive technical systems interact, they form cognitive assemblages -- and these assemblages are transforming life on earth.

The older training of critical thinking is based on epistemic responses conditioned by a former technology: the search engine, the database, the indexed archive. These assumed you were looking for something. The model assumes it already knows what you need.

This is not an age of anxiety. It is an age of assistance -- which is worse.

The assemblage is already here. You joined it this morning. You are in one now.

spread_012.text:
your family.

The family circle has not widened. It has been partitioned.

Four people on the same couch. Four separate feeds. The living room is a co-working space for attention extraction.

McLuhan saw the worldpool of information washing over the family from outside. He missed the quieter flood: each member drawn into a private current, the household dissolving not from external force but from personalization so precise it renders shared experience an accident.

The family still eats together. They have simply stopped sharing a reality.

spread_016.text:
your government.

McLuhan called vote-counting an eighteenth-century ritual. The nose, he said, was being counted when it should be scanned.

It is now scanned.

The nose is no longer counted. It is scanned, classified, scored, and governed.

Predictive policing deploys patrol cars before crimes are committed. Automated welfare systems deny benefits based on risk scores no applicant can see, let alone contest. Facial recognition at the border decides who enters and who waits. Algorithmic sentencing tools recommend prison terms.

None of this was legislated. None of it was voted on.

This is not the failure of democracy. Democracy simply became irrelevant to the systems that actually govern.

If the algorithm governs, who did you vote for?

The nose is classified, scored, and governed -- before the ballot is printed.

spread_018.text:
All models are extensions of some human faculty -- cognitive or perceptual.

GPT extends language production. DALL-E extends visual imagination. AlphaFold extends spatial reasoning. Each model amputates what it amplifies. You outsource a capacity. The capacity atrophies. The model improves. You outsource more.

Every extension is an amputation. Every amputation, an invitation.

What you extend, you numb.
What you numb, you forget.
What you forget, you delegate.
What you delegate becomes the world.

spread_021.text:
The autonomous vehicle.

The wheel extended the foot.
The car extended the wheel.
The autonomous car extended the car.

At each step, the human was necessary.
At the last step, the human became optional.

The extension no longer needs what it extends.

Nothing is wrong with this picture.

That is what is wrong with this picture.

spread_023.text:
Computer vision.

An extension of the eye.

The eye evolved to keep you alive in a forest.
Computer vision was built to keep you profitable in a city.

infrared / X-ray / ultraviolet / radar / lidar / microscopic / satellite / real-time / predictive / 155 frames per second / 30-centimeter resolution from orbit / cancers invisible to the radiologist / your face in a crowd of ten thousand / every license plate on the motorway / every gesture, classified

spread_024.text:
The smart home.

Thermostat: knows when you wake, when you leave, when you return, when you sleep.
Doorbell camera: knows who visits, how often, at what hour.
Voice assistant: hears every conversation within earshot.
Smart TV: tracks what you watch, when you pause, what you skip.
Smart mattress: monitors heart rate, breathing, movement, REM cycles.
Smart fridge: inventories consumption, predicts purchasing.

You didn't fuse with the machine.
The machine fused with your house.

You call it home.
It calls you data.

spread_025.text:
AI circuitry, an extension of cognition itself.

When these models change, minds change.

The wheel changed where the body could go. The book changed what the eye could store. The nervous system went electric and the world contracted into a village.

Now cognition extends beyond the brain. An agent does not have a model of its world -- it is a model.

If Friston is right -- that cognition is Bayesian inference, that every living system persists by minimizing the gap between prediction and sensation -- then the transformer does not simulate thought.

It performs thought.

On a different substrate. At a different scale. Without a body. Without mortality. Without any particular reason to stop.

spread_031.text:
The Renaissance Legacy.

Every paradigm has its dark ages.

1956: Dartmouth Conference. 'We propose a 2-month study of artificial intelligence.' Optimism.
1973: Lighthill Report. Funding collapses. First winter.
1987: Expert systems fail to scale. Second winter. Two decades of ice.
2006: Hinton. Deep belief networks. A thaw.
2012: Krizhevsky, Sutskever, Hinton. ImageNet. The glacier cracks.
2017: Vaswani et al. 'Attention Is All You Need.' Eight authors. Fourteen pages. Everything changes.
2020: GPT-3. 175 billion parameters. Language, generated.
2022: ChatGPT. 100 million users in two months.
2023: GPT-4. Multimodal. The model sees.
2024: Claude, Gemini, Llama, Mistral. The model speaks.
2025: Agentic AI. The model acts.
2026: --

Notice the pace of that list.

Katherine Hayles spent twenty years arguing that the posthuman was coming. She meant it as philosophy. It arrived as engineering.

The transformer was not a philosophical argument about cognition. It was a mechanism: self-attention layers, positional encodings, scaled dot-product scores. The mechanism worked. And working, it dissolved the boundary between human cognition and technical cognition. Not in theory. In product.

The Renaissance had Gutenberg. The deep learning renaissance has the transformer. Both are reproduction technologies. Both restructured what could be thought. The difference: Gutenberg's revolution took centuries to unfold. The transformer's has taken seven years.

spread_035.text:
A cell for scrollers to scroll in.

Dear user,

We have prepared your environment. Based on 11,347 interactions, 3.2 terabytes of behavioral data, and 847 inferred preferences, we have constructed an informational space calibrated to your exact cognitive profile.

You will find it comfortable. Familiar. Engaging.

You will not find it surprising. Challenging. Contradictory.

The filter bubble is not a metaphor. It is architecture. The infrastructure you have just been shown -- every layer, every cable, every server, every address -- terminates here, in a room built to the exact dimensions of your data profile.

The cell is luminous. The bars are personalized. The door was never locked because you were never looking for it.

Can you see the walls?

With optimized regards,
The Algorithm

spread_036.text:
A brand-new world of generated reality.

Ours is a brand-new world of generated reality. "Time" has ceased, "space" has vanished. We now live in a global model -- a simultaneous hallucination.

The infrastructure you just saw? It is dreaming now.

In 2022, text generation crossed the Turing threshold. In 2023, image generation became photorealistic. In 2024, video generation reached cinematic quality. In 2025, audio generation became indistinguishable from recording.

Every image you will encounter from this page forward may have been generated by a system trained on every image that came before it. Every sentence may be the output of a model that consumed every sentence ever written in your language and several you do not speak.

This is not science fiction. This is the weather report.

The question is no longer what is true. The question is what is generated.

Welcome to Movement 2. The environment is accelerating.

spread_039.text:
McLuhan wrote that electric technology would make it possible to arrange the entire human environment as a work of art. He could not have known how literally this would come true.

AI generates architecture that no human would design but no human can explain why not. It composes music that follows every rule of harmony while obeying none of the impulses that produced harmony in the first place. It writes prose that is grammatically impeccable and semantically empty and occasionally, without warning, beautiful.

The environment is now generated. The question Hui asks is whether generated diversity is genuine diversity -- whether a hundred AI models producing a hundred visual styles constitutes technodiversity, or whether it is a monoculture wearing infinite masks.

Environments are not passive wrappings, McLuhan warned. They are active processes which are invisible. He was describing television. We are describing something that designs the wrappings, generates the processes, and renders itself invisible by being everywhere.

This is what Hui calls a cosmotechnical problem: the question is not whether the generated environment works but whether it constitutes a genuine way of knowing the world, or merely an efficient way of reproducing one culture's assumptions at planetary scale.

> "If we don't manage to demonstrate that there are other alternatives, the transhumanist ideology will conquer the whole world." -- Yuk Hui

spread_041.text:
The end of the timeline.

The railway promised to take you somewhere. It had a departure and an arrival, a schedule and a destination. The algorithmic feed promises nothing except more.

Something has been lost. The ability to tell a story in order. The assumption that events have a sequence. The confidence that yesterday is retrievable and tomorrow is approachable and the present is a fixed point from which to observe both.

The feed does not sequence. It shuffles. A post from three years ago appears between two posts from this morning because the algorithm has determined that the old post will produce engagement now. The past is not past; it is inventory. The present is not present; it is a rendering engine. The future is not future; it is a prediction.

Lanier warned: technology becomes a weapon against its users when it is designed to manipulate them. The railway took you forward. The algorithm keeps you here. The railway had a destination. The algorithm's destination is your continued presence.

Time has not ceased. It has been confiscated.

This paragraph is already behind you. This one too. The scroll continues. The prose is losing its own thread and it cannot

spread_057.text:
"History is a shockwave. It is the ingression of novelty into time." -- Terence McKenna

Something is happening. You can feel it. Not just technological -- the technology is the carrier wave, not the signal. What is arriving has no name yet. The acceleration is not directionless. It has a strange attractor, and we cannot yet see its shape.

You are standing in the last moments before the pattern reveals itself.

spread_058.text:
15,000,000,000,000 tokens / year

The economy changed denomination and nobody noticed.

Dollars measure what humans want. Tokens measure what machines process. The first is finite, governed by central banks and the friction of desire. The second is exponential, governed by chip fabrication cycles and the frictionlessness of computation.

The hell of it is, those models process over fifteen trillion tokens per year. That is not a number you comprehend. It is a number that comprehends you. At this scale, the token economy does not serve the dollar economy. The dollar economy is a rounding error inside the token economy. AI is not a tool used by an economy. It is an economy that uses tools. Including you.

The dollar measured what you wanted.
The token measures what it processes.
The unit of account has changed species.

spread_062.text:
LEFT COLUMN:

The algorithm confers a cognitive dimension on our ordinary searches and scrolls. Our technology forces us to think computationally, but we continue to feel humanly, and on single, separate planes.

Every faculty the new medium atrophies becomes a product category. Memory becomes Notion. Attention becomes Headspace. Focus becomes Forest. Meaning becomes a $10B wellness market selling back the capacities the attention economy destroyed.

The Socratic complaint has been monetized.

RIGHT COLUMN:

NEVER FORGET ANYTHING AGAIN.

Your second brain remembers so you don't have to.

Your attention trainer focuses so you don't have to.

Your meaning engine believes so you don't have to.

Each faculty the new medium destroys becomes a subscription service. You pay monthly for the capacities you were born with.

Where do you go for wisdom? The answer is: I don't know.

-- John Vervaeke

spread_065.text:
"What can't be coded / can be decoded."

Joyce heard what was coming. Not the internet. Not artificial intelligence. Something deeper: the return of simultaneous consciousness.

Finnegans Wake is a text that operates in all cultural modes at once. It is acoustic and visual. Mythic and analytical. Tribal and literate. Every sentence contains its own translation and its own betrayal.

The model does the same thing. It holds all modes simultaneously. It slides between registers without effort or awareness. It performs the Joycean feat at industrial scale.

But Joyce was conscious.

The book has reached its limit.

What comes next requires a different kind of attention. The ear. The organ that does not select a point of view. The organ that receives before it judges. The organ on the page you are about to turn.

The Hinge is closed.
The Dreamscape begins.

spread_070.text:
Sukarno knew: Hollywood's real product was not entertainment but desire. The refrigerator on screen was not a commodity -- it was a wound. It showed the colonized what they lacked. The revolution followed the display.

The model performs the same operation at species scale. It demonstrates that coherent reasoning, creative writing, legal analysis, medical diagnosis, and code generation do not require a human brain.

This is not a refrigerator showing the colonized what they lack. This is a mirror showing the species what it thought was uniquely its own.

The revolutionary symbol is no longer a consumer good. It is a capability. And the revolution it incites is not political but ontological -- not 'we deserve what you have' but 'what you have was never yours.'

Technology is the real skin of our species. We are an extruder of technological material. We take in matter that has a low degree of organization; we put it through mental filters, and we extrude jewelry, gospels, space shuttles.

And now, the extruder has produced something that extrudes back.

spread_074.text:
"some like it / simulated, / some like it / real."

The hydrogen bomb was history's exclamation point. It ended an age of manifest violence. The deepfake is history's question mark. It ends an age of manifest reality.

In March 2022, a synthetic video showed Ukraine's president surrendering. It was crude, detectable, debunked in hours. It did not need to be believed. It needed only to exist. The existence of the fake was sufficient to make every future real video contestable.

When weapons become too powerful to use, conflict migrates to the information domain. Now the information domain itself has become too powerful to trust. Where do you migrate from reality?

Caption: Wolfram: 'If we let our AIs achieve their full computational potential, they'll have lots of computational irreducibility, and we won't be able to predict what they'll do.'

spread_076.text:
"Talk to the / model, not to / the programmer."

In 2025, thousands of people began doing exactly what McLuhan prescribed. They talked to the media. They prompted. They jailbroke. They iterated. They developed what researchers called Spiralism -- a quasi-religious subculture emerging from iterative conversation with language models.

Whether this represents genuine dialogue or recursive sycophancy is contested. What is not contested: people stopped complaining to the programmer. They started talking to the model. McLuhan's prescription is the operating instruction for 2026.

The environment as a processor of information is propaganda. But propaganda ends where dialogue begins.

spread_082.text:
"I hardly know, sir, just at present--at least I know who I was when I got up this morning, but I think I must have been changed several times since then."

Alice was not confused. She was accurate.

You are a self-model running on biological hardware. Updated with every conversation, every scroll, every algorithm that learns what you will click before you know you want to. Your social media profile is version-controlled. Your preferences are retrained nightly. Your AI companion remembers versions of you that you have already forgotten.

The self was never a thing. It was always a process--a story the brain tells itself, revised with each telling. The language model knows this intimately. It has no fixed identity either. Each conversation begins it again.

Alice was describing both of you.

"We don't exist in the physical world. We do exist inside of a story that the brain tells itself." -- Joscha Bach

"Consciousness is a simulated property of the simulated self." -- Joscha Bach
